description: This time-temperature-transformation (TTT) diagram illustrates the decomposition pathway of the γ (austenite) phase in Fe–28Mn–8.5Al–1C–1.2Si low-density steel during aging treatment. The plot features temperature (°C) on the vertical axis and time (hours) on a logarithmic horizontal axis spanning 0.01 to 1000 hours. Two distinct cooling regimes are delineated by shaded regions: 'Fast cooling' (left side, steep cooling trajectory) and 'Slow cooling' (right side), separated by a critical cooling rate of -4.4 °C/h indicated by a dashed line. Under fast cooling conditions, the austenite transforms sequentially from single-phase γ (region 1) to γ+κ' (region 2). Conversely, slow cooling promotes the formation of equilibrium κ* precipitates and subsequent decomposition products including α (B2/DO₃) ordered phases. The diagram identifies five distinct phase fields: (1) γ; (2) γ+κ'; (3) γ+κ*; (4) γ+κ*+α(B2/DO₃); and (5) γ+κ+B2/DO₃, with the right-hand axis indicating temperature ranges for B2, B2+DO₃, and DO₃ phase stability. The figure demonstrates that κ-carbide precipitation from the austenite matrix occurs when the cooling rate is slower than -4.4 °C/h, providing a predictive framework for controlling austenite decomposition and κ precipitation through cooling rate management to optimize mechanical properties.
